zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean Le Mercier於1555年10月18日出生在法國巴-諾門迪奧內時，他的父親Jehan de Cocquiel27歲，母親Peronne de la Houe21歲。他於1579年10月10日與Jane Le Clere在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和10個女兒的父母。他於1582年在英國哈普爾素坦普頓被列為he was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton.。他去世於1626年1月3日在英國哈普爾素坦普頓享年70歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jean Le Mercier于1555年10月18日出生在法国巴-諾門迪奧內时，他的父亲Jehan de Cocquiel27岁，母亲Peronne de la Houe21岁。他于1579年10月10日与Jane Le Clere在英国英格兰哈普爾素坦普頓结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和10个女儿的父母。他于1582年在英国哈普爾素坦普頓被列为he was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton.。他去世于1626年1月3日在英国哈普爾素坦普頓享年70岁，埋葬在英国英格兰哈普爾素坦普頓。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Mercier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Mercereau
meanings: French: occupational name from a diminutive of Old French mercier ‘merchant’ (see Mercier ). Compare Mersereau .

History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered form Mersereau) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Marshall
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.
Americanized form of German   Marschall  .
Americanized form of French   Mercier  .

variants.name: Mercy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jean Robert.
Altered form of French   Mercier  .
French: habitational name from any of the places so named in Allier, Meuthe-et-Moselle, and Yonne, derived from the Latin personal name Marcius, Martius, or Mercius + the locative suffix -acum.

variants.name: Mercer
meanings: English and Catalan: occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, merchier (see Mercier ). In Middle English the term was applied particularly to someone who dealt in textiles, especially the more costly and luxurious fabrics such as silks, satin, and velvet.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

variants.name: Jeane
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jean .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: Edict of Nantes
historicEvents.description: Under the rule of Henry IV of France the Edict of Nantes was issued giving the people some religious liberty. The edict brought an end to the religious wars that were going on at the time.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: Reigns of Louis XIII and Louis XIV. Royal power at its peak. France dominates Europe, French culture spreads. Start of large-scale sea trade.

historicEvents.title: 30 Years War
historicEvents.description: Ferdinand II was trying to force the people to follow Catholicism. The 30 years war was a battle of religion with the Protestants and Catholics, in the Holy Roman Empire.

en.lifeSummary: When Jean Le Mercier was born on 18 October 1555, in Orne, Lower Normandy, France, his father, Jehan de Cocquiel, was 27 and his mother, Peronne de la Houe, was 21. He married Jane Le Clere on 10 October 1579, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 10 daughters. In 1582, at the age of 27, his occupation is listed as he was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton. in Southampton, Hampshire, England. He died on 3 January 1626, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, at the age of 70, and was buried in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean Le Mercier est né le 18 octobre 1555, à Orne, Basse-Normandie, France, son père, Jehan de Cocquiel, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Peronne de la Houe, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Jane Le Clere le 10 octobre 1579, à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 10 filles. En 1582, à l’âge de 27 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est he was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton. à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 3 janvier 1626, à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean Le Mercier am 18. Oktober 1555 in Orne, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jehan de Cocquiel 27 und seine Mutter Peronne de la Houe 21. Er hat am 10. Oktober 1579 in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom Jane Le Clere geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 10 Töchter. 1582, als er 27 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als he was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton. in Southampton, Hampshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 3. Januar 1626 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Southampton, Hampshire, England und wurde in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean Le Mercier è nato il il 18 ottobre 1555 nel Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Jehan de Cocquiel, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Peronne de la Houe, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Jane Le Clere il 10 ottobre 1579, nel Southampton, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 10 figlie. Nel 1582, all'età di 27 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come he was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton. nel Southampton, Hampshire, England. Morì il 3 gennaio 1626, nel Southampton, Hampshire, England, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Southampton, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean Le Mercierが1555年10月18日にフランスバセ-ノーマンディオルンで生まれた時，父Jehan de Cocquielは27歳，母Peronne de la Houeは21歳であった。1579年10月10日にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアソウサンプトンでJane Le Clereと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と10人の娘の親であった。1582年，27歳の時，イギリスハンプシュアイアソウサンプトンでの職業はhe was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton.と記録されている。彼は1626年1月3日にイギリスハンプシュアイアソウサンプトンで70歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアソウサンプトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean Le Mercier(이)가 1555년 10월 18일 프랑스 배세-노먼디 른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jehan de Cocquiel(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Peronne de la Houe(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1579년 10월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 Jane Le Clere(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 1582년, 27세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 he was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton.이었다. 그는 1626년 1월 3일 영국 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴에 묻혔다.

pt.18 October 1555–3 January 1626: 18 de outubro de 1555–3 de janeiro de 1626
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean Le Mercier nasceu em 18 de outubro de 1555, em Orne, Basse-Normandie, França, seu pai, Jehan de Cocquiel, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Peronne de la Houe, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane Le Clere em 10 de outubro de 1579, em Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 10 filhas. Em 1582, com 27 anos, sua profissão aparece como he was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton. em Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 3 de janeiro de 1626, em Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean Le Mercier родился 18 октября 1555 г. в местности орн, бассе-норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Jehan de Cocquiel было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Peronne de la Houe, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Le Clere 10 октября 1579 г. в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 10 дочерей. В 1582 году, в возрасте 27 лет,  он работал по профессии he was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton. в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия. Он умер 3 января 1626 г. в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания.

sm.BET 1550 AND 1555: mai le 1550 e o'o I le 1555

es.18 October 1555–3 January 1626: 18 de octubre de 1555–3 de enero de 1626
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jean Le Mercier nació el 18 de octubre de 1555, en Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Jehan de Cocquiel, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Peronne de la Houe, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Jane Le Clere el 10 de octubre de 1579, en Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 10 hijas. En 1582, a la edad de 27 años, su ocupación registrada era he was the attorney for his father-in-law, arnoul le clercq in 1582. he was an import/export merchant in southhampton. en Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra. Murió el 3 de enero de 1626, en Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
